name: Budgeted best-first (rooted PCST approximation)
description: Self-contained eval port of the parent's budgeted-best-first strategy. Starts at a single root type (Query) and grows a connected subgraph by repeatedly picking the highest-scoring affordable field candidate under a token-budget cost model. Candidates are FIELD edges out of already-included types; their score blends cosine^2 with a lex bonus (BM25-/trigram-style overlap on field names). Cost varies per candidate: cheap for scalars/known-target types, expensive for fresh object types. Slice is naturally Query-rooted so no paths-to-root closure is needed by default.
